title: valeriana excelsa subsp. transiens
intro: Valeriana excelsa subsp. transiens is a subspecies of Valeriana excelsa, a plant species native to the mountainous regions of New Guinea. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including its sedative and anxiolytic effects, which are attributed to the presence of valerenic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Valeriana excelsa subsp. transiens include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are prepared by steeping the dried roots and leaves in hot water.
